title: iochroma horridum
intro: Iochroma horridum, also known as the "Purple Flower of the Andes", is a flowering plant species native to South America, particularly in the Andean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Iochroma horridum can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
